selected_important_turn.text: Hi! I really need water also. I'm trying out a few new recipes, and they call for a lot of water.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This response immediately turns a cooperative opening (THEM proposed splitting water) into a competitive claim over the same top-priority resource. It raises the zero-sum stakes early and shapes the rest of bargaining around contested water.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: As phrased, it simply asserts competing need without acknowledging the other party's proposal or offering a path to share the resource. That escalates rather than enabling the integrative suggestion the opponent made and misses a chance to preserve cooperation while protecting your interest.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Show shared interest and move quickly to a constructive trade: acknowledge the proposed split, state your need, and either ask to try a split (e.g., "splitting water sounds good — can we try 1/1?"), or propose an explicit trade-off that compensates you for taking less water (e.g., more food/firewood). Framing as a cooperative trade rather than a competing claim would open integrative options.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: likely increase — opening to a cooperative split or trade preserves more value-creating options than a direct competitive claim
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — clarifying a balanced split or trade reduces perceived zero-sum grabbing and supports fair concessions
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — a cooperative framing makes it easier to reach agreement quickly rather than provoking back-and-forth competition
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — acknowledging the other's proposal and proposing a concrete cooperative next step builds trust and rapport

value2reason.Low: I don't want my fire to burn out during our overnight!
value2reason.Medium: I'm a big guy and need a lot of calories -- it can make planning the right amount of food difficult because I'm always hungry.
value2reason.High: I can get really thirsty after doing all my outdoor activities.

value2reason.Low: We recently cut down a dead tree and brought the wood with us.
value2reason.Medium: I might need some extra in case the soup doesn't work out.
value2reason.High: I am going to try to make a soup recipe that I found, so I need to boil a bunch of vegetables.

dialogue.text: Hi! I'm really hoping to get a lot of water. I love outdoor activities like hiking and kayaking so I'll need to stay hydrated on the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Hi! I really need water also. I'm trying out a few new recipes, and they call for a lot of water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m willing to offer 2 Water, 1 Food, and 1 Firewood if you take the remaining 1 Water, 2 Food, and 2 Firewood. It’s a fair split based on our priorities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Splitting water sounds good — can we try 1/1? I need more for my soup, but I’d be happy to trade some food or firewood if you’re okay with that.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m okay with splitting water 1/1, but I need more food for my energy. How about I take 1 Water, 2 Food, and 1 Firewood, and you take 2 Water, 1 Food, and 2 Firewood?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
